---
title: "{{< meta titles.full >}}"
---

{{< include /docs/includes/_badges.qmd >}}

This package it pretty simple: Have a common package where we can put
functionality that we use across many of our Python packages. Rather
than copy and paste code that we use across are packages, we hoist them
over into our "soil" to provide a foundation to "grow" those other
packages.

While we apply a strong design and documentation-first approach to
building our Python packages, we don't use this approach in Soil. That's
because most of the design happens in the other packages where the
functionality is initially developed. As we develop these
functionalities in those other packages and find that our other packages
could use them them, that's when we move them over into Soil.

For that reason, Soil doesn't have a strong "scope" or "vision". It's
more of a "toolkit" for us.
